body_txt: Some characters have outrageous, gravity-defying hairdos . Then there's characters like these, with only two or three strands of hair. To a casual viewer, a character with Charlie Brown Baldness will appear, well, bald. However, the character may occasionally mention getting a haircut, or other characters will comment on said haircut or hair colour. The Trope Namer is Charlie Brown from Peanuts (pictured), who according to Word of God has very short, light blond hair (keep in mind Charlie's father is a barber, and that was a popular style at the time the strip was created), but seems to be completely bald except for a bit of curly hair on his forehead. In parodies, expect jokes about the character suffering from cancer , especially if the character is a child . Contrast Furry Baldness , where an animal character is "bald" despite still having hair, feathers, or fur, and Prematurely Bald where a character is actually bald despite their young age.
